articles.title: NOT EVERY DISRUPTOR WEARS A HOODIE
articles.summary: Some wear grit, results — and a NASDAQ ticker.

articles.title: OPENAI OPERATOR UPDATE
articles.summary: o3 Operator, OpenAI's CUA-powered browser agent, has replaced its previous GPT-4o-based model.

articles.title: NVIDIA TO LAUNCH CHEAPER BLACKWELL AI CHIP FOR CHINA AFTER US EXPORT CURBS, SOURCES SAY
articles.summary: Nvidia plans to launch a new artificial intelligence chipset for China at a significantly lower price than its recently restricted H20 model. The company has scheduled mass production to begin as early as June. The new chips will be priced between $6,500 and $8,000, well below the $10,000 to $12,000 the H20 sold for. The lower price reflects its weaker specifications and simpler manufacturing requirements.

articles.title: ATTENTION WASN'T ALL WE NEEDED
articles.summary: A lot of modern techniques have been developed since the original 'Attention Is All You Need' paper. This article looks at some of the most important ones developed over the years and tries to implement the basic ideas as succinctly as possible using the Pytorch framework.

articles.title: AN MCP-POWERED AGENT IN ~70 LINES OF CODE
articles.summary: Hugging Face has extended Tiny Agent design to Python, using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to streamline tool integration for LLMs.

articles.title: FM-INTENT ENHANCES NETFLIX RECOMMENDATIONS
articles.summary: Netflix's FM-Intent is a hierarchical multi-task learning model that improves recommendation accuracy by modeling user session intent from implicit signals.

articles.title: INFINITE TOOL USE
articles.summary: Large language models should never output anything but tool calls and their arguments. Exclusively working through tools allows models to externalize large parts of their intelligence to more efficient domain-specific programs. Some developers are already switching to a tool-use paradigm, but this is still limited to very short contexts and to only parts of the model output. This post provides examples of how the tool-use paradigm benefits different domains, like text editing, video understanding, and 3D generation.

articles.title: TRAINING-FREE AGENT FOR APP AUTOMATION
articles.summary: GUI-explorer is a training-free agent that autonomously explores and extracts knowledge from mobile app interfaces using unsupervised methods. It improves task success rates without retraining.

articles.title: BENCHMARKING SPATIAL UNDERSTANDING IN MLLMS
articles.summary: SpatialScore is a new multimodal benchmark suite for evaluating 3D spatial reasoning in large models. It integrates 28K samples from 12 datasets.

articles.title: GENAI'S ADOPTION PUZZLE
articles.summary: ChatGPT usage grew amazingly fast - much faster than PCs, the web, or smartphones. However, it was just a website - people didn't need to go buy a new device or wait for infrastructure to be deployed. Despite its advantages, ChatGPT's daily active users to weekly active users ratio is pretty bad. This may fix itself over time as models mature and more use cases emerge. The chatbot interface might only be working for some people and some use cases, so it might be better to wrap features and capabilities inside other things.

articles.title: ORACLE WILL BUY $40B IN NVIDIA CHIPS FOR OPENAI'S STARGATE
articles.summary: Oracle will purchase about 400,000 GB200 chips for the Stargate data center in Abilene, Texas.

articles.title: IT'S HARD TO MAKE SCHEMING EVALS LOOK REALISTIC FOR LLMS
articles.summary: Claude 3.7 Sonnet easily detects when it's being evaluated for scheming on the Apollo's scheming benchmark.

articles.title: TV SHOWCASES GOOGLE'S VEO AI VIDEO CAPABILITIES
articles.summary: Flow TV continuously streams user-generated AI video clips, and their associated prompts, organized into thematic channels.

articles.title: GEMMA 3N ARCHITECTURAL INNOVATIONS - SPECULATION AND POKING AROUND IN THE MODEL
articles.summary: A quick look at Gemma 3n, a new member of the Gemma family with free weights that was released during Google I/O.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC AND LEGENDARY MUSIC PRODUCER RICK RUBIN PUBLISH MANUSCRIPT ON VIBE CODING
articles.summary: Based on Lao Tzu's Tao Te Ching, “The Way of Code” is a meditation on humility and creativity while vibe coding featuring interactive AI-generated visualizations.
